acceptance_targets: [chapter_09_prose_negation_ladder_tic] Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:211, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:317, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 no longer present more than two instances of the same "Not X / Not Y / corrective beat" construction across the chapter, and each passage can be read as carrying a distinct emotional rhythm; fail if three or more of those beats still use the same negation-ladder shape.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:211, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:317, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 no longer present more than two instances of the same "Not X / Not Y / corrective beat" construction across the chapter, and each passage can be read as carrying a distinct emotional rhythm; fail if three or more of those beats still use the same negation-ladder shape.
findings.evidence: The same negation-ladder sentence shape recurs at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:141 ("Crowded. Not tragic. Not poetic."), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:211 ("Not for his hand. Not that."), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:217 ("Not love. Not anything that clean."), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:255 ("Not startled. That would have been easier."), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:317 ("Not as policy. As cost."), snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:319 ("Not relief. Recognition, maybe."), and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:325 ("Not a yes. Not tonight.").
findings.problem: Once this many emotional beats are built with the same "Not X / Not Y / short corrective" pattern, the prose stops feeling chosen and starts feeling automated. The repetition flattens distinct moments of grief, desire, bargaining, and recognition into one reusable cadence, which is exactly the kind of recurring construction the review brief says to flag.
findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence architecture in the beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:141, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:211, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:217, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:255, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:317, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:319, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:325. Keep the emotional distinctions, but make those passages arrive through different rhythms and clause structures rather than repeated negation ladders.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the novel's uncomfortable overlap of sanitation, state violence, labor dependence, and family love. When an edge case appears, prefer the version that keeps physical consequence, class coercion, and emotional contradiction visible on the page rather than the version that makes characters cleaner, nobler, or easier to forgive.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene turns away at the exact moment bodily, economic, or moral consequence should become legible, revise toward specificity. Omission is only valid when residue, aftermath, and changed behavior keep the pressure fully present.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms should mark emphasis, bureaucracy, class performance, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate dense procedural chapters with quieter aftermath, meals, night holds, confession scenes, and dark-comic institutional theater. No two consecutive chapters should rely on the same rhythm of scene count, dialogue density, or pressure delivery; vary between confrontation, drift, revelation, ritual preparation, humiliation, public spectacle, and stillness under pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, infrastructural, unsentimental, and specific about procedures without becoming jargon-drunk. Abstract thought should rise from physical consequence instead of replacing it.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third anchored primarily to Marisol's bodily labor, practical thought, and moral self-management, with any widening kept chapter-level and earned by scene design.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Let long freighted sentences carry labor process, institutional language, or accumulating sensory load; then cut to shorter, grammatically complete sentences at impact points where a body, fact, or decision can no longer be blurred.
